312 E Duke of Gloucester St, Williamsburg, VA 23185, USA
Black walnut trees line each side of the street. Access: Public.
Lots of blackberry brambles here. Many large berries, very delicious. Seems to be thriving as of early July 2023. The patch is threaded throughout the unmowed area. Falling Fruit season: July-July.

Wild blackberry patch; not sure what kind, but they're fairly small in size, though numerous. They're growing on the road side of the ditch. Ripe as of the very end of June/early July 2023. Falling Fruit season: June-July.

Plenty of autumn-olive plants tangled around the base of trees. Not sure what their fruiting status is, I haven't yet seen them in fruiting season. Based on other plants in the area, that would be around October (not sure how long the season is). No one lives

Several pokeweed plants. FYI, do NOT forage these unless you're experienced, they can be deadly poisonous when not prepared correctly. 

Don't eat the berries, they're always poisonous.

Small pawpaw grove on both sides of the street. No sidewalks or places to park, so it's better to bike here or to part somewhere nearby and walk. Most of the trees are not fruiting (either too young or not pollinated), but there's a tree near a lightpost that
Grove of pawpaw on the side of the road (all along the corner). Not the Shenandoah variety Falling Fruit season: August-September.
